detailed_analysis: daemon.py is the Layer-4 polling driver: signal registration, a cancellable poll loop, an error-budget counter, and structured logging. It is small (108 lines, well under the 300-line module limit), single-responsibility, and the public function watch() is 64 lines of body but low in branching. There are no CRITICAL findings: the one broad `except Exception` is deliberate fault-isolation (FR-02 / US-07 AC-3) and is logged at exception level with a traceback before incrementing the failure counter, so it is NOT C3 silent-swallowing. Signal handlers are registered defensively for cross-platform support. The only substantive code-quality observations are: (a) watch() exceeds the project's 40-line function guideline at ~47 non-blank body lines and carries the loop, the error budget, and signal wiring in one function (mild M2 / SRP); (b) the architecture doc claims daemon.py is '37 lines' in two places (§2, §4) while the file is 108 lines — a stale documentation fact, not a code defect; (c) the watch() docstring documents exit codes 0 and 1 but the function reuses NFR-10's tri-state codes only partially (config-error exit 2 lives in cli.py, which is correct layering). Naming is intention-revealing throughout (request_stop, consecutive_failures, max_consecutive_failures). No hardcoded secrets, paths, injection vectors, mutable defaults, or no-op bodies. Conformance to US-07 AC-2/AC-3 and NFR-10/NFR-13 is sound.

findings.problem: watch() body runs ~47 non-blank lines, exceeding the project's 40-line function guideline (AGENTS.md §3). It bundles three concerns: signal-handler wiring (lines 60-69), the poll/error-budget state machine (lines 71-104), and termination logging. Cyclomatic complexity (~8) is under the >10 threshold and the logic is readable, so this is a guideline overage, not a true M2 defect. Could extract the per-poll outcome classification (lines 74-91) into a helper. No correctness impact.

deviations.description: Doc states daemon.py is '37 lines' (§2 layer-4 description and §4 module map). The actual file is 108 lines. Stale documentation fact about the audited module; not a code defect, but the architecture artifact no longer describes the code truthfully (architecture doc requires any change to leave it 'either true or amended').

verdict_summary: Clean Layer-4 driver; one INFO function-length overage and one stale line-count in the architecture doc; no CRITICAL/MAJOR/WARNING findings.
